sustainability strategy 58 Refrence list 60 appendix 63 Research Proposal Research relevance “AK SK”   is a n  architectural bureau based in Saint-Petersburg.  It performs projects of all complexities and work s  with  well-known  construction companies such as “LSR” and “ Geozol ”.   The bureau has 15 employees in state .  The modern expectations on corporate sustainability at companies are rising and take on an international dimension .  Complete ignorance of such agenda will eventually destroy the company competitiveness.   The relevance of this paper is justified by the fact that  more global organizations  are having concerns raised from inefficient resource management and the lack  of corporate,  social,  and economic  responsibility  needed from  business. Over the last decade the society formed a certain set of expectations regarding sustainable development and long-term benefit s .  The resources are  s carce,  and it is essential to be dynamic and  prepared  to adopt to  new realities . The transition  may be seen as an additional cost but can become a justified investment and will help to gain a competitive advantage for the  company . Managerial Problem Definition Managerial problem is defined by several factors. On the first place,  it’s company awareness of economic,  social,  and ecological issues appeared on global scale. Company’s attempts to solve problems which are beyond just receiving financial profit  set the Russian company “AK SK” closer to compliance with international expectations and standards. Secondly,  Integrating  sustainability development strategy prepares the company to enter international level and form a competitive advantage on the market.   Goal   Development of a corporate sustainability strategy for the “AK SK” architectural bureau  Objectives: give a company overview define current business model describe industry characteristics introduce theoretical background  for sustainable development and sustainable design in architecture find SDG and impactful activities  understand materiality of topics for stakeholders and external environment  indicate macro environment for architecture service industry define the level of capability for integrating corporate sustainability strategy analyze the  micro environment  which influence the market define the current sustainability level at the company demonstrate the quant itative  sample after integrating sustainable design frameworks provide business model for improving sustainability level summarize final recommendations and  give  conclusion Object and subject The object – “AK SK”.  The subject –  sustainable development strategy .  Research method ology Interconnection between methods and explanation for usage To determine whether there i s an optimal  corporate sustainable  strategy for   “AK SK ” , it is necessary to  determine : Company’s  current business model Customer experience Industry specifics Value chain at “AK SK” Materiality of sustainable development topics Sustainable development in architecture Macro and  micro environment Product Pricing Cost Structure Company’s business sustainability level To get the description  about the company business model and its product pricing ,  the interview with  CEO and head architect will be conducted .  The value chain and the logic model will be used to   analyze  what company’s activities impact  SDG the most and what are the inputs, output, and outcomes from activities.  Furthermore,  interview with top management will help to determine the materiality of sustainability topics and the company impact on that topic .  PESTEL analysis will be applied to determine the macro environment which affect the architectural service industry.  Sustainable SWOT analysis is used to determine the capability for integrating the corporate sustainability strategy potential opportunities and threat which will help to form recommendations. 5 Porter’s Forces is applied in this research to understand the  microenvironment  and evaluate the potential risks for  corporate sustainability strategy development. Using the real cases from the company’s portfolio is used to determine the current business sustainability level. The sustainable business canvas is used as a framework for identifying the areas in which the company can improve and shift higher in the level of business sustainability. Finally, applying all the conducted analysis tools and business sustainability   level   analysis   and sustainable business canvas will help to provide a  conclusion for recommendation on corporate sustainability strategy. Chapter 1: Company overview and Industry characteristics Company’s background   Company History The “A K  SK” ltd is an architectural bureau which mainly focuses on  develop ment of  project documentation for objects of any complexity, undergo ing  an examination, issue working documentation and carry out architectural and construction supervision .  It was founded in 2017 by Smolin Nikolay  Arkadievich . The founder of the company is a well-known figure at Russian architect community. For example, Nikolay  Arkadievich  was involved in projects such as  Scientific and educational complex "ALMAZOVA ", new building of “ Mariinsky  Theatre” and “Lotte Hotel” in Saint-Petersburg. The founded bureau is a full reflection of Smolin’s expertise and working principles. Therefore, he managed to make not just an architectural company but the environment where young an d  ambitious architects can implement complex projects under the supervision of highly skilled professional. Before launching an independent company, Nikolay  Arkadievich  was working as a senior architect at “Studio 44”. It is one of the leading organisations in this segment which offers a wide range of services such as  conceptual design , d esign of transport facilities, large technologically complex public buildings (objects of culture, healthcare, sports, leisure, trade, etc.) , r econstruction of historical buildings, restoration and adaptation of cultural heritage sites , d evelopment of all constructive sections of the project , d evelopment of all engineering sections of the project, including external networks and structures on networks.  “Studio 44” is well known for “ Mikhailovskaya  dacha ” for Saint Petersburg State University, restoration, and   modernisation   of   the   public   library in name of  M ayakovsky , reconstruction and restoration of museum complex of the state heritage in the east wing of the General HQ.  Company structure The segment where “AK SK” operates has its features in terms of  organizational  structure which are widely practiced among many architectural bureaus. The main product of such company is a project development; therefore, it requires a high intellectual resource. The primary goal is to  organise  a project team and only after such formation the company may allocate resources on operation management. In case of “AK SK” it has 15 employees where 14 of them are architects including senior architect Nikolay Smolin and CEO  Konovalov  Nikita  Urevich . CEO at “AK SK” has responsibility of a Financial and Operational director. He is also involved in customer relationship. Senior architect also serves the role of an HR as he has an expertise to evaluate the professionalism of an architect candidate. All incoming project requests are spread between all  architects and senior architect is involved in all working projects. The company can take 2-3 projects at a time. The timeframe varies from customer, but the ordinary project is usually  completed in 2 months.  Organizational structure Business model The firm is mainly responsible for AR (Architecture) direction in the project. AR is one of the 11 sections in design documentation for capital construction facilities for industrial and non-industrial purposes. To better understand bureau specifics, it is essential to apply business canvas. Business Canvas Customer segment The architect bureau operates in B2B and B2G sectors. Mainly, “AK SK” receives requests from building companies and industrial enterprises. Furthermore, it completes orders for State institutions such as hospitals, state buildings, and educational institutions. Governmental requests are the significant projects on the country level such as construction of an Olympic village or reconstruction of the Airport.  “AK SK” regularly does AR projects for “LSR”, “ Graphit ” and “ Geoizol ”. LSR actively attracts “AK SK” as a subcontractor for AR and in addition for  development of a section on ensuring access for persons with disabilities  (EAPD) . The specificity of AR section implies the inclusion of EAPD. Value proposition To serve best to its customer segment “AK SK” by following strict adherence to project timelines. All sections of the project must be done in time and should not exceed the acceptable time deviation.  “AK  SK” puts this value in high priority and always in time with all deadlines. Secondly, rich experience of main architect Nikolay Smolin ensures that the project quality is at highest standards. In architect bureau business name and reputation serves as a best marketing tool. Moreover, “AK SK” is capable to supervise all project stages and independently attract subcontractors for constructive and engineer  calculations. Key activities The architect bureau is primarily focused on project documentation development. It includes concept formation, layout planning and design. The key activities are a good indicator of a current company direction which can be used to identify the strongest areas of the company and develop a track for activity’s expansion. Core expertise at “AK SK” is a project documentation development.    This includes objects of any complexity. Documentation can be developed for industrial buildings such as factories or for objects which require  special technical conditions (ST C ) .  Moreover, the company conducts architectural  and construction supervision.  Supervision is a control of all sections of the project. If AR section dictates the project development, “AK SK” ensures that all other sections such constructive and engineering follow the guidelines and do not step away from architectural concept. Finally, “AK SK” helps with passing examinations at state or commercial organs. This depends on the projects whether it belongs to state or commercial purposes. “AK SK” helps to prepare documentation and pass all documentation examinations. This is essential in order to receive approval on the project from the construction committee. Key resources The key resource at “AK SK” is HR. The bureau provides a product which is produced because of using architect experience and knowledge. This quality combination is considered as the main resource of the company. Any intellectual service is keen on developing and expanding HR as a key resource. Furthermore, the next key resource for “AK SK” is undoubtably the name  and reputation. To fully deliver the value preposition to its customer segment the company carefully replenishes the portfolio with new projects and gain reputation in architecture sphere. Key partners   The company actively interacts with different institutions and committee to implement an architectural project. The bureau interacts with  Committee for Urban Planning and Architecture (CUPA)  and  Committee for State Control, Use and Protection of Historical and Cultural Monuments  (CSUPM). This contact is essential on different project stages for receiving an agreement. For instance, agreement on  compliance with architectural standards   in cases of renovation, where it is significant not to break historical heritage.  The next key partner, for “AK SK” is a constructive and engineering subcontractor. The bureau currently focuses only on “AR” section, therefore, is attracted as a subcontractor for the project. If the project is allocated fully to “AK SK” the company is capable to find additional engineering and constructive bureaus. However, more often the company is invited as a part of an existing team. Moreover, when the company finalises the documentation, it interacts with construction committee. This is done to receive an agreement on construction processes. More likely, the responsibility is on customer side but in cases when “AK SK” supervises and helps to pass examination, it directly communicates with the committee. Customer relationships “AK SK” aims to build a close relationship with its customer. Firstly, every stage of the project is agreed with a client. Sometimes, bureau builds an indirect relationship. It appears in cases when building company has an affiliate body which building company interests. Therefore, “AK SK” may not have a direct relationship with the final customer, however, it creates a close relationship with its representatives.  Channels One of the main channels for reaching the customer service is participation in tender platforms open or closed. Such platforms provide the scope and specificity of the project. The company prepare the application with the offer and send it either electronically or by mail. After being selected as a contractor “AK SK” will start the working process.  The second main channel is the word of mouth. The architect bureau market is relying on earned reputation.  Occasionally, “AK SK” receives project requests because of recommendation of their previous customers. Currently, the bureau is actively working with “LSR” group as a subcontractor.  Cost structure “AK SK” bureau has the main category of costs which form the cost structure of the product. Firstly, it is operational costs which includes salary fund and office rent. This may also include procurements of computers and professional software purchases.  Revenue streams The company only receives revenue from provided services such as documentation development and the project section which holds under “AK SK” responsibility. Furthermore, as the company provides passing examination it also receives an additional revenue stream. The net profit from provided service ranges from 10-20%. Customer experience The customer experience with “AK SK” starts with the request incoming from the customer to make a project documentation and find general designer and subcontractors for the project. To place the inquiry the customer may use tender platforms or attract entities to the project directly. If company places a tender lot. It has a detailed information about requirements and contractors with the best conditions receive a lot. In another case company may have heard about “AK SK” or has already worked with the bureau and will attract the architect company without tender conditions. Before starting to develop documentation “AK SK” estimate the complexity of the project and discuss all details with the project seeker. When the exact timeframe and all details are discussed the architect project team starts to design the project and develop documentation. Intermediate approvals are involved at different stages of project development. In case when “AK SK” is a subcontractor and not a general designer of the project. Bureau passes examination from general designer and finalises the results. When “AK SK” is appointed as general designer it will be responsible in coordinating subcontractors and regularly communicating with the client. Theoretical background behind sustainability development and sustainable architecture To conduct analysis and develop the set of recommendations, it is necessary to build theoretical background. Corporate social responsibility and business sustainability The concept of corporate social responsibility started to arise at the beginning of the  21th  century. The next industrial  revolution brought company founders such as Henry Ford who started to implement first corporate sustainability strategies. The company high productivity was achieved by prioritising the working employees. The working day was reduced by an hour from 9 to 8 hours of working time and establishment of a new waging system which stimulated labour to achieve sustainable efficiency. This is a significant moment in time as it appears to be a starting point for corporate social performance and business sustainability.  The time was passing and understanding of  CSR was transforming. Before moving  to Donna  Wood concept  (1991)  it should be noted that there is a logical transition from just performance to overall corporate responsibility.   The Wood’s concept was strongly affected by  A.Carroll . and CSR pyramid. A new  concept  in compariso n states that corporate social responsibility is a set of actions and not just a single activity.  Moreover, high attention is paid to the resulted impact rather than working performance. To summarise, it should be said that Wood’s  vision was keen on  more sociological direction rather than eco nomic and managerial. As a result of her work managers can apply framework by using straight forward logic . Wood’s corporate social performance (CSP) model Business sustainability The last milestone which formed the modern understanding of business sustainability was introduced by  Dyllick  and Muff in article “ Clarifying the Meaning of Sustainable Business: Introducing a Typology  from  Business-as-Usual to True Business Sustainability ”. The authors  suggest  3 types of business sustainability.   Business Sustainability 1.0 (Advanced Shareholder Value Management), Business Sustainability 2.0 (Managing for Triple Value) and Business Sustainability 3.0 are different types of business sustainability (True Sustainability).   Business Stability Typology Business-as-usual mainly focuse s only on economic firm and viewing the company only from economic perspective. B.S 1.0  recognises new economic challenges  from exchanges which are outside the market.  Extra-market challenges result from environmental or social concerns which are typically voiced by external stakeholders like NGOs, media, legislation, or government. They raise  environmental and social concerns that create economic risks and opportunities for business. Business Sustainability 2.0 brings beyond understanding of 1.0. 2.0 broadens the stakeholder perspective and pursuing a triple bottom down approach. It lets business to align the concerns it addresses with the values it seeks to create by relating economic,  environmental, and  social concerns. 2.0 means to produce and report measurable results  within  clear sustainable development areas and at the same time doing it in economically and profitable manner . Triple bottom line  =( people, planet,  profit). Lastly, Business Sustainability 3.0 or Truly Sustainable Business. This model reflects a much broader sustainability questions such as “how can business contribute with its products and services to resolve pressing sustainability issues in their societies” or “how can business use its resources, competencies, and experiences in such way as to make them useful for addressing some of the big economic, social, or environmental challenges?” To conclude, it should be said that 3.0 is the final step in the concept of  Dyllick  and Muff. The company considers sustainability issue from outside and  thinks how it can contribute to the goals. The next section will be dedicated to the goals which the company may set to achieve. Sustainable development goals (SDGs)  The concept of sustainable development is generally recognised as a concept of enhancing productivity by replacing the used resources with alternatives of equal or greater value without degrading natural biotic system . This practice aims at solving social, economic, environmental, and political concerns. The sustainable development goals (SDG) were defined by Global Reporting Initiative, United Nations, and  World Business Council for Sustainable Development .  There are 17 main goals : no poverty zero  hunger good health and well-being quality education gender equality clean water and sanitation affordable and clean energy decent work and economic growth industry, innovation, and infrastructure reduced inequalities sustainable cities and communities responsible consumption and production climate action  life below water life on land peace, justice, and strong institutions partnerships for the goals SDG does not only involve state institutions and non-governmental organisations but also businesses. The companies are starting to prioritise the goals by contributing to SDG. Moreover, it does not only consider ethical aspects of sustainability but also long-term benefits.   Companies recognise that achieving sustainability in different areas will result in following consequences : business opportunity expansion (sustainability applications on growing markets ); enhancing the value of corporate sustainability ( e.g.  strengthening economic incentives to use energy more efficiently); stabilisation of social and economic  environment ; common language communication (SDG defines a framework for companies and creates a universal set of actions. Therefore, companies will more consistently communicate with stakeholders about the impact and performance). For achieving sustainable development goals, it is essential to be in partnership with business as the contribution comes from their core activities and redefining the value chain. Before moving to  framework applications , it is necessary to define the concept of sustainable architecture. Sustainable design Sustainable architecture or green architecture is based on sustainable development principles. The term was initially introduced in 1993 in Declaration of Interdependence for a Sustainable Future. The main idea of this document was to introduce commitment to all architectural and building related entities that : environmental and social responsibility should be placed at core of professional responsibilities to achieve continual improvement on  practices, procedures, products, curricula,  services  and standards that will enable the implementation of sustainable  design  (SD) education and populate the concept among building, construction and architectural companies is significant to adopt SD policy establishment is and practices in government and business will ensure and populate SD bring all existing and future elements of the built environment such as design and production to sustainable design standard will ease mass adoption Over the years of adopting the concept of sustainable architecture, architectural bureaus formed a new vector for development. For instance, taking into consideration the sun light as the alternative energy. Fossils fuels and natural gases have limits and do to promote long-term healthy leaving; therefore, solar energy can be seen as a beneficial alternative. Second significant milestone in SD architecture is indoor air improvement. This can be explained by the fact that the average person spends more than 50% of the time indoors and indoor air can be more polluted than outdoor. This can be achieved by improvements in ventilation and filtering system. Thirdly, high attention is paid to efficient land-use. This strategy involves things such as  utilizing  less land for construction but allocate more land on recreational purposes. Moreover, it should be noted that apart from efficient energy use and attentive land allocation, sustainability is also achieved by high-performance architectural design. This principle describes the idea to waste as little as possible, therefore, finding the perfect balance between comfort and conservation. Finally, the wise use of natural materials. Natural materials are finite and should be used with responsibility.   The wise use of natural resources includes timber management, use of recycled materials, and the development of new synthetics that can be safely used without harming the environment. To systemise the sustainable design approach in architecture the architect’s resort to several frameworks which will be used in next sections of sustainable development strategy.   The framework has 3 main levels such as principles, strategies, and methods. Conceptual framework for sustainable design  Principles are based on resource conservation, life cycle design and design for human adaption. Conservation’s strategies focus on energy, water, land, and material conservation. There is a high concern with reduction and reuse of resources. When the strategy is identified, it is essential to choose methods to implement the chosen strategy. For instance, energy conservation can be tackled by choosing materials with low embodied energy or solar energy and land conservation can be solved by energy-conscious urban planning.   Strategies and methods to achieve resource conservation  Life - cycle   design   has   a   different   approach .  This approach recognises the environmental consequences of the whole life cycle. The cycle is divided into 3 strategies which are pre-building, building and post-building. The pre-building phase includes selection of material and design which have an environmental impact. The methods suggest using recycled materials, choosing materials with long life and low maintenance or use natural materials which considered are less toxic for the environment. The building phase has differed in methodology from pre-building phase. Firstly, this phase is keen on design factors such that it will promote longevity and flexibility of an object. Flexibility in this sense corresponds to adoption of the building to new needs and requirements. Finally, post-building phase involves examination of the outlived structures. It involved material recycling from a building. This process is often difficult  due to difficulty in separating different substance from one  another.  For example,  glass and aluminum, must be scavenged from the building by  hand . However, s teel can easily be separated from ruble by magnets and concrete can be crushed and used as aggregate in new pours. Strategies and methods to achieve life-cycle design The last key principle that should be mentioned is human health and comfort protection. It focuses on quality by providing healthy and comfortable environment for the human activities. The concept of design for human adoption is separated into two parts: Protecting human health and confront and protecting physical resources. To achieve human health and comfort achieved by designing thermal and acoustic comfort, spread of daylight. Moreover, the architects pay attention to methods such as overall aesthetics of the building and its functionality. Aesthetics plays an important role in psychological comfort. On the other hand, p rotecting physical resources  is one of the most important principles of sustainable design and construction.  It touches the issue of building resilience from disasters such as earthquakes, fire-incidents, flooding, or crime attacks. The set of methodology involves fire protection and crime prevention designs.  Passive fire protection in building can be achieved by use of insulated fire-resisting partitions, cavity barriers, specialist fire-stopping of gaps in structure with their proven fire performance properties. Beside that architects can design escape routes in some building in order to allowing the occupants escape from fire.   Crime prevention design develops three main approaches  natural access control; natural surveillance; and territorial behavior .  Access control uses doors, shrubs, fences, gates, and other physical design elements to discourage access to an area by all but its intended users. Surveillance is achieved by placing windows in locations that allow intended users to see or be seen while ensuring that intruders will be observed as well. Surveillance is enhanced by providing adequate lighting and landscaping that allow for unobstructed views. Finally, territory is defined by sidewalks, landscaping, porches, and other elements that establish the boundaries between public and private areas. These three methods work together to create an environment in which people feel safe to live, work, travel, or visit . S trategies and methods to achieve human adaptation  After brining introduction to sustainable architecture design, it is important to conclude that each principle has a slightly different focus. Following one sustainable development principle will not fully reflect the concept of sustainability. It is to consider all principle at the stage of the architectural project. All necessary features of suitable architectural designs were briefly explained and now the question is rising how to develop understanding of sustainability, define priorities with goals, as well as sustainability integration.  SDG’s compass is a commonly wide concept for business use.  This guideline managed to create a methodology which can be applied to all types of businesses. The first stage of the guideline is based on understanding and familiarizing SDG. After when the horizon of goals is clear it is essential for the company to set priorities from 17 goals to which it has a direct relation. For selecting the priorities, the companies often use value chain map to identify impact areas. For instance, Life Cycle Assessment (LCA) or environmentally extended input-output (EEIO) to analyse the whole company and its activities and impact on environment. When priorities are identified, the company selects indicators for measuring the current and future states of impact. The next step is goal setting which is based on the outcomes from impact assessment and prioritisation. Goal setting is defined by the scope and KPI selection as well as baseline for the goal and the ambition. The level of ambition for the goal setting is important as if the company set the goal which is higher that its current performance and does not have a complete  solution it will incentives creativity and will spur innovation. As a result of setting the KPI and goal setting, it is now the stage of integrating sustainability into core business processes. It is important to correctly adjust the SDG within the business and spread the sustainability across all functions. Finally, continual improvements on SDG integration will lead to the process of reporting and communication. The reporting material issues caused by integration whether it had a positive impact on economic, environmental, or social indicators. Interview clearly showed that all eight topics are relevant to the company and it is worth building the baseline for the company responsibility.  Baseline responsibility Architectural bureau representatives highly value its impact and recognises the importance for solving sustainable challenges. Baseline responsibility should be used as a factor of recognition and should be set after prioritisation and integration. Good Health&Wellbeing Consequences of inefficient design and project construction significantly affect the human health and wellbeing. The positive contribution to this matter can be achieved through the project design. Sustainable design which focus on enhancing not only human health and comfort but also improving energy efficiency and action towards climate change. Good health and wellbeing is directly represented by one of three main principles in sustainable design which corresponds to design form human adaption. This principle focuses on human comfort protection and physical resources. Human comfort is based on thermal, acoustic, daylighting, aesthetic and functionality aspects. All of those factors are important at any type of project design and ,therefore, evaluated as an important goal for SK. Sustainable cities and communities This goal was highly ranked as it directly corresponds to architectural services. The opportunity to design spaces which promote sustainability concept should be taken into prioritisation. Companies are starting to set new standards which response to current concerns. It was realised that supply for real estate market is inelastic and may not be simply reduced to meet the demand. Therefore, properties become vacant or abandoned. This does not simply harm the economic environment but creates pressure on cities and communities. Architectural bureau is responsible for reconstruction and concept reshaping of real estate. In such sense this is impactful for the business and for achieving SDG. Life on Land L ife on land is highly valuable for SK and the whole real estate system. Development industry observe a consequence of material scarcity which increase the construction costs and negatively impact the environment. Capital market also have similar concerns and form a new set of expectations upon investors. As a result, the goal opens an opportunity to switch the natural material to recycled alternatives. This is a material conservation principle which is covered by sustainable design framework. Protection terrritorial ecosystems generates a new ecological agenda as well as the opportunity for business to adopt to new needs.  Industry, innovation and infrastructure   Infrastructure development is a part of any design project. The influence of architectural service market on this factor is vital and was rated as an impactful and important for the company. The possible baselines to achieve this goal is to integrate new methods to project development stage and take more innovative customer orders Clean water and sanitation This goal represent not only the resource conservation methods but also improvements in human life’s and comfort. Public expectations are following the new standard for urban living. Piping and filtration systems have achieved a significant improvement to meet new needs fro water cleaning. According to the sociological research conducted by  RANEPA  15,5% of the whole population prefer healthy and active lifestyle . In comparison, it is 3 time more than it was in 2010. The correlation  between the healthy lifestyle and sustainability development  in real estate is the fact that more people realise the importance of life longevity and long-term health benefits. One of the main aspects of sustainable design is to make the people’s life better and create healthier and more comfortable spaces. In January 2022, the demand for goods from the category of "conscious consumption" increased in Russia by 3.2 times compared to the same period last year. At the same time, the demand for products  labelled  "eco" and "ecological" increased by 3.5 and 9.6 times, respectively. ATOL Online, through which every second online purchase in Russia passes, noted that in 2021, Russians bought goods  labelled  "eco" for 5.7 billion  rubles . In January 2022, this figure reached 613 million  rubles .   New trend in conscious consumption spread triggers the wider adoption of eco-lifestyle (living, traveling, product consumption, recycling and etc) . Technology Innovations in 3D printing.Additive manufacturing can print entire buildings using durable materials, such as concrete or green alternatives. This construction method minimizes waste, resulting in lower costs and carbon footprint. 3D printing can create an entire house in a matter of days by autonomously printing the walls using a preprogrammed floor plan. A small crew then adds the roof, interiors and other finishing details.   For instance,  one of the  case s   was presented  in 2017 in Yaroslavl by “ Spetsavia ”.   The first residential building in Europe and the CIS, built using construction 3D printing technology. Construction of the house began in 2015. The building box was printed piece by piece by a portal printer and then mounted on the foundation in one month in December 2015. The  latest technologies in construction which strongly influence the sustainability development trend is the creation of carbon concrete.   This is a new modification of reinforced concrete, in which carbon  fibres  and tubes are used instead of conventional metal reinforcement. They are not inferior in strength to metal, while gaining in lightness and ductility. The carbon concrete developed specifically for facades with solar panels.  Secondly, the creation of self-healing concrete  which  can protect itself from destruction due to the presence of calcium lactate and some types of bacteria in its composition.  Those examples  is  a clear evidence that there is a  technological development which stimulates the movement in sustainable architecture development. Environment in 2021 air pollution increased on 23% .   According to experts, last year in Russia there were 369 cases of high and 37 cases of extremely high air pollution . 25 cases of accidental pollution were also identified, twice as many as in 2020.    The rising air pollution stimulates many concerns related to sustainability agenda.  Ecological issues create not just a tension for the government but the great opportunity for private sector to establish initiatives and new business model which  attempts to solve such issues. Russia has collected 30  billions  of waste from which less than 40% can be recycled .   According to  Rosprirodnadzor  data for 2018, the total production of waste of I (extremely hazardous) and II (highly hazardous) hazard classes is 278.1 thousand tons: 21.9 thousand tons - class I, 256.2 thousand tons - class II.   Construction materials belong to those groups . During deconstruction of building less than a half of all  components can be reused or recycled. This raises a need towards sustainability design. Legal Domestic standards for green certification.  It was initiated due to current inability to certify new objects with international standards such as BREEAM , LEED. Currently,  there are about 10  domestic  certification systems, among them new ones: Green Rating - a new Russian method for assessing the sustainable development of companies and a "green" standard for rating certification of real estate; Eco Smart Office is a voluntary certification system for office  centres  and co-working facilities  based on the LEED CI international certification and Green House is a voluntary certification system for residential real estate objects based on the BREEAM international certification.   However, it can be said that there  are  not much stimulation from the government side which will  create a greater motivation for conducting sustainable projects such as tax reductions or more certification benefits.   For instance,   all  successful foreign examples (California, Florida and Massachusetts in the USA,  Britain  and France in Europe) indicate that green building and modernization are spreading in countries with clear benefits for specific owners, whether it be a tax deduction, a subsidy for the purchase of equipment or modernization or mortgage rate cut . Conclusion PESTEL analysis helped to understand the current environment  and what is the current state of sustainability. However, the Bank of Russia established special conditions  for providing mortgages for “eco-real estate”. Furthermore, more people started to take the healthy lifestyle more seriously and started to be more conscious in consuming products. The technological improvements are starting to cover the real estate system and overtime may reduce the total costs. Overall, the environment is evaluated favourable for developing the corporate sustainability strategy, but current geopolitical and economical state add an additional risk. Business  sustainability level analysis Correlation between business sustainability level and architecture service industrial  characteristics Correlation between architectural industry and business sustainability Sustainability   level / charectiristics Sustainable   design Business  operations Business as usual Legal minimum No sustainable development practices Business sustainability 1.0 Part of the framework. Adopt to customer needs Recognises  the concerns beyond economic,  Tries to implement some sustainable development practices  Business sustainability 2.0 Core of the design framework Business sustainability practices are core part of the company Business sustainability 3.0 Self-sufficient beyond customer requirements All business operations are  organised  to solve sustainable development issue and require more company’s effort  The architectural services  are  a specific business field which require some templates for identifying the level of business sustainability the company has. Business as usua l  is the level where the architectural company only measures profitability indicators and uses the minimum legal elements of sustainable design and does not have any sustainability development practices at the business process level. Business sustainability 1.0 uses the sustainable design to the extent of the customer desires and requirements  for the project design. However, it does not integrate the principles on the core level. In terms of business operations, it starts to recognise not just economic  concerns,  but also social and environmental risks and challenges caused by its business activities. Business sustainability 2.0 is a transitional stage before being truly sustainable business. This level of the organisation already implemented the sustainable design to its core designing process as well as the business operations. The organisation on this level  establishes  partnerships with eco oriented construction and development firms and becomes an active contributor  to sustainable development plan. Business sustainability 3.0 is a shift towards realisation that the company not just simp ly provide sustainable design required by the customer but creating more requirements from itself. The company at this level is only able to efficiently cooperate with eco-oriented companies.  To  clearly  identify what sustainability  level,  the company has  it would  be   appropriate to recall the theoretical introduction by  Dyllick  and Muff and operate with results from  sSWOT .   Business sustainability is divided into different levels. To directly  remove irrelevant levels the real company’s cases will be used to prove  an absolute mismatch.  SK architectural bureau does not belong to the level of “business as usual. As the company does not put in priority the economic concerns (cheap materials, reducing costs and increasing profits). Business Sustainability 1.0 can be considered closer to the current state of “AK SK ”   business  sustainability. 1.0 level is the company recognition of new challenges such as  environmental and social and at the same time carefully evaluating the economic risks. The SK architectural bureau will differ from  most of  the business cases as it corresponds to challenges not by the company governance but by their product. To recap,  “AK  SK” follows the level 1.0 based on  Dyllick  and Muff framework. As it was identified in the PESTEL analysis the consumption power for purchase the private real estate is currently slowed down and very sensitive to price increases. Furthermore, the biggest number of newly constructed objects with applications to  suitable  design are office spaces , commerce, and warehouses. It clearly demonstrates the demand for spaces which challenges environmental and social issues form the business side. More business is getting awareness of responsibility and long-term stability of their business activities. Even though there is not clear and transparent subsidy system for business, the Bank of Russia is currently offering lowered mortgage rates for purchasing eco-private real estate which will compensate the appreciation of the eco-building. Therefore, it can be said that estimations on new price calculations should be carefully considered at the  decision-making  process  of deciding the economic profits and risk agenda. The external environment slowly but moves towards stimulation of sustainable development agenda. Sustainable business canvas  Rearrangements o f the business model will be based on the finding s  from conducted analysis.  Positive impact (Maximise) The  company  has a  wide spectrum of positive impacts caused by its activities. The end-product of SK is a construction object. The result of using sustainable design  positively impact human wellbeing, climate change,  resource conservation and  creation of sustainable cities and infrastructure.  The small sized company can solve significant economic, social, and environmental concerns. Bureau is part of real estate system with many entities Architectural bureau is influential especially on the planning stage . It directs the whole design process from which developers and construction firms make design the reality. The outcome of key company’s activities  is  discussed  in detail at SDG compass section.  To maximise the positive impact, it is essential to put sustainable design on the core level of project design but not just apply it as a framework for separate projects. Negative impact (Minimise) The are several activities which lead to a negative impact. Firstly, the  project documentation requires a high paper consumption.  Thi s contradicts the sustainability concept . The paper is used for drafting design and final documentation.  It is impossible to completely remove the paper consumption at the  company,  but the volume can be minimised.  The first step towards paper usage reduction is to fully transpose design processes to digital spaces.  Usage of cloud services may be considered as the way of sharing the project and create an efficient working environment for project management.  The second step  is to build a relationship with a customer an give an opportunity to choose a preferred method for sharing project documentation. The sustainability agenda should be recognised  by both stakeholders and be bound by shared values. Sustainable value proposition To move towards the new sustainability level  2.0 , it is not enough to simply apply the sustainability design frameworks. It is more importantly to recognise the importance and the impact of the completed work. This should be integrated to  core value propositions. Firstly, at the early stage of the project design it essential to recognise the significance for increasing the living standards , to contribute to sustainable city and infrastructure development,  take part in climate action and attempt to improve the energy efficiency. Transforming those goals to values is the first step for recognising the concerns which  are outside the company’s boarders Sustainable value creation (key activities) In addition to the main business activities  the company recognises the that sustainable design is an essential activity derived from newly set of values. Furthermore, SK  must  be keen on finding the partnerships and be an active member in sustainable development  community.  Sustainable Tech and resources The new resource that should be used for 2.0 level achievement is the sustainable design framework. Without this method it will not be possible to  reach the value propositions.   Secondly is the usage of new types of software. There is a wide variety of software which solves the problem of carbon neutrality  management ( “Green Wizard”) , LEED standard guidance   (“United Design  Construction Suite  Green”)  and programs for  resource  conservation ( “ Autodesk  Green  Building  Studio”) .  Sustainable partners In addition to existing partners the company should redefine the relationship with  committee of nature management and ecology safety. The new relationship should not be limited by only applying for certification and approvals, but active negotiations related to new carbon emission standards in construction. Moreover, the partnerships with domestic NGO’s can be come an opportunity to find out about new concerns and focus on triple bottom line approach. Sustainable customer relation The customer relationship stays the same as it does not impact the level of sustainability. Sustainable channels Existing channels such as  tender platforms and existing clientele network should be kept with no change. The  channels  at SK is not high impact sustainability area. Responsible customers To shift to 2.0 the company does not have to completely change its customer segment. The company still operates in B2B and B2G market. The easier cooperation  would be achieved with “green” oriented companies which are beyond the level “business as usual”. End of Life It can be  calculated  the exact resilience of a built object. However,  it can be said that buildings certified with LEED status are  more resilient and can be recertified at the end of the exploitation period .  Sustainable design  simplifies  the reconstruction processes and aims and minimising the overall pollution and ease the recycling processes by using alternative materials. Cost structure Additional costs To existing cost structure, there is an additional cost related to software  purchases for “green design” and monitoring the total impact on environment. It is an important title in the cost structure as the architectural companies purchase software licensing on subscription bases and, therefore, it is inevitably  affecting  the total service prices. Secondly,  the cost structure will be changed due to more complicated constructor and engineering works. The sustainable design  makes it more challenging to conduct not standard calculations related to recyclable and reusable materials and engineering solutions related to resource conservation and more efficient energy use. This estimately adds from  15-20%  additional cost. Subsidisation As it was underlined in the  sSWOT  analysis there is a lack of government stimulation which is one of the potential threats. This leads to the problem that there is no strong subsidisation for the architectural bureau which follows  SDG. On governmental level there is a deep recognition related to “green construction” and  sustainability issues. There is a set of initiatives which are planned to underline the benefits of real estate system stakeholders to move the industry towards eco-construction . Revenue & Sustainability Premium At 2.0 level the company still will consider the completion of the project the  only  revenue stream. Sustainable business model canvas Final recommendations  on corporate sustainability strategy In this section of the research paper, it will be summarised and suggested the final recommendations and guidelines for shifting the business level sustainability even further.  By revision the initial business model and conducting the analysis in chapter  2 the key elements were underlined: The most impactful area of the company’s activity is its product The company is  adviced  to integrate the suitable design frameworks in core project activities  based on its new value propositions  To shift the company business model  from 1.0 to 2.0 SK should revise the current business model and  make sustainabl e design a core framework at the company Begin to cooperate  with  eco-oriented construction firms  Establish a relationship with domestic NGOs for supervising the sustainable issues in the area The corporate sustainability strategy is not a  static set of long-term actions for achieving SDG. Integrating sustainable development frameworks into core company’s activities is only a first steps understanding its importance  and social and environmental impacts. The Russian market steadily forms a modern set of expectations which are broader than economic risks. The stakeholders are setting a  new benchmark  which do es  not challenge domestic issues but  global. The developed corporate sustainability strategy is actual response to rising concerns. This is not only an investment into the  long term  stability but a formation of a global competitive advantage.
